From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor

| Michael Durrell | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | Sylvester Salvatore Ciraulo[[1]](#cite_note-1)   [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), U.S. |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 1969–present |

**Michael Durrell** (born **Sylvester Salvatore Ciraulo**) is an American actor.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Durrell&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Durrell began his career in the role of attorney Peter Wexler on the [CBS](/wiki/Columbia_Broadcasting_System "Columbia Broadcasting System") soap opera *[The Guiding Light](/wiki/The_Guiding_Light "The Guiding Light")*. In 1969, he appeared on Broadway in *Cock-A-Doodle-Dandy* at the Lyceum Theatre. Other television roles were as [police](/wiki/Police "Police") [Lieutenant](/wiki/Lieutenant "Lieutenant") Moraga in the short-lived CBS [crime drama](/wiki/Crime_drama "Crime drama") *[Shannon](/wiki/Shannon_(1981_TV_series) "Shannon (1981 TV series)")* (1981–1982), starring [Kevin Dobson](/wiki/Kevin_Dobson "Kevin Dobson") in the title role, and then as Nicholas Stone from 1984–1985 on CBS's *[Alice](/wiki/Alice_(American_TV_series) "Alice (American TV series)")*.

Another well-known role was in 1983 in the [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") science fiction miniseries *[V](/wiki/V_(1983_miniseries) "V (1983 miniseries)")* and the 1984 sequel *[V: The Final Battle](/wiki/V_(The_Final_Battle) "V (The Final Battle)")* as Robert Maxwell; he reprised his role in the first two episodes of *[V: The Series](/wiki/V_(1984_TV_series) "V (1984 TV series)")*. He played [D.A.](/wiki/United_States_Attorney "United States Attorney") Lloyd Burgess on the hit TV series *[Matlock](/wiki/Matlock_(1986_TV_series) "Matlock (1986 TV series)")* from 1986 to 1990, and as Dr. John Martin, the father of Donna Martin on the hit [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") TV series *[Beverly Hills, 90210](/wiki/Beverly_Hills,_90210 "Beverly Hills, 90210")*, in which he had a recurring role. He guest starred in the *[Star Trek: Deep Space Nine](/wiki/Star_Trek:_Deep_Space_Nine "Star Trek: Deep Space Nine")* episode "[Sanctuary](/wiki/Sanctuary_(Star_Trek:_Deep_Space_Nine) "Sanctuary (Star Trek: Deep Space Nine)")". He appears in Season 1, Episode 5 of *[The Eddie Capra Mysteries](/wiki/The_Eddie_Capra_Mysteries "The Eddie Capra Mysteries")* and Season 5, Episode 10 of *[Dexter](/wiki/Dexter_(TV_series) "Dexter (TV series)")*.
